title: Clarkia rubicunda

The species is further classified as a member of the subgenus Godetia, which is characterized by its unique flower shape. The Onagraceae family is a group of flowering plants commonly known as evening primroses or willow herbs. Clarkia rubicunda is a rare and endangered species found in California and Oregon.

The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its potential to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory function, and exhibit antioxidant and antimicrobial activities.
